title: Investigate test:ci fixture resolution failure
description: Local npm run test:ci fails before suite execution on macOS with Cannot find module '/Users/akira/LaTeX-Secure-Workspace/test/fixtures/unittest'. Investigate why the VS Code test runner is attempting to execute the fixtures directory as a module entrypoint and restore CI-local test execution.
close_reason: Fixed the integrated-terminal env leak that made nested Electron launches inherit ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE and VSCODE_* and try to execute test/fixtures/unittest as a module entrypoint. Remaining local failure is tracked separately in LaTeX-Secure-Workspace-gnd.

title: Add repository security policy
description: Add SECURITY.md for coordinated vulnerability disclosure using develop@toppymicros.com as the current contact, with clear scope, response targets, and safe-harbor language.

title: Bundle extension to reduce VSIX size
close_reason: Reduced VSIX size safely without trimming language support

title: Fix hover trust boundary and PR npm install hardening
Investigate background launch for Extension Development Host on macOS
description: Users running extension tests from VS Code still see the Extension Development Host briefly foreground on macOS even with debugger focus suppression. Investigate whether we can offer a truly background/no-activate launch path for local test runs without breaking exit-code handling or debugging.

title: Investigate full test:ci timeout in 06_structure after unittest suite
description: Running npm run test:ci outside the sandbox now launches VS Code correctly, but the full sequence still fails in the testground pass: Document structure test suite times out in the before-all hook for [075] 06_structure: basic structure when run after the unittest suite. The same 06_structure suite passes when isolated with LATEXWORKSHOP_SUITE=06_structure. Investigate shared state leakage or workspace/test fixture reuse between suites.

title: Cut v11.1.0 stable release
description: Bump package version to 11.1.0 on master, create tag v11.1.0, publish GitHub release, and verify stable release automation.

title: Pursue external security posture follow-ups
description: Track optional follow-up work that lives outside direct code scanning remediation: enrolling the repository in OpenSSF Best Practices, continuing to land reviewed PRs so Scorecard CodeReview improves organically, and revisiting dismissed policy alerts if project policy changes.

title: Validate custom-editor PDF rendering in VS Code runtime
description: Verify that the custom editor tab now renders the PDF body after switching from direct viewer.html injection to the existing iframe-hosted PDF.js path. Capture [viewer-host], [viewer-bootstrap], and [viewer] logs from the LaTeX Workshop output channel if the PDF is still blank.

title: Register repository on OpenSSF Best Practices Badge site
description: Create the bestpractices.dev project entry, complete the questionnaire, and then add the live project-specific badge URL to README.md.

title: Resolve remaining Scorecard policy alerts after code-scanning cleanup
description: Track the repo-level Code Scanning alerts that are not closed by code changes: BranchProtectionID, CodeReviewID, FuzzingID, CIIBestPracticesID, MaintainedID, and any remaining PinnedDependenciesID items in devcontainer Dockerfiles. Handle them via repository settings, policy, or separate dependency pinning work.
close_reason: Code scanning remediation completed; remaining policy items were addressed or dismissed with rationale.

title: Add E2E coverage for secure viewer forward SyncTeX
Re-enable forward SyncTeX integration coverage
description: The TeX-to-PDF tab SyncTeX regression is now covered by unit tests, but the end-to-end integration suite remains skipped because the PDF viewer suite is unstable in self-host tests. Revisit the minimal PDF viewer harness and restore a deterministic integration test for forward SyncTeX scrolling.
close_reason: Re-enabled forward SyncTeX integration coverage and hardened the tab viewer runtime so TeX-to-PDF reveals/scolls reliably in the custom editor.

title: Investigate unresolved brace-expansion 1.x advisory under minimatch@3 tooling deps
Add Open VSX release publishing
Stabilize forward SyncTeX tab viewer test in CI
description: Daily Release has been failing on GitHub Actions because test/suites/05_viewer_synctex.test.ts times out in the Verify daily release build step on ubuntu-latest. Investigate whether the forward SyncTeX custom editor tab test is flaky or missing a readiness signal, then implement the smallest fix that restores daily release verification.

title: Polish minimal PDF viewer controls and activity icon
close_reason: Added zoom controls to the minimal PDF viewer and refreshed the activity bar icon.

title: Raise OpenSSF visibility and best-practices readiness
description: Add Scorecard badge visibility, improve repo metadata/docs for OpenSSF Best Practices, and enable any safe repository settings that help scoring and badge readiness.
close_reason: Added Scorecard badge visibility, community docs, and GitHub issue/support metadata for OpenSSF readiness.

title: Remove orphaned Math Preview and viewer SyncTeX files
description:
## Summary
Remove unreachable legacy files tied to the retired Math Preview panel and dead viewer SyncTeX component while keeping compatibility no-op commands and settings intact.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Delete the orphaned Math Preview panel source and web asset
- Delete the unused viewer SyncTeX component
- Update references/docs so no deleted file path is mentioned
- Confirm compile and test pass after cleanup

## Notes
Remaining mathpreviewpanel settings and disabled commands stay for compatibility and should be handled in a separate follow-up if desired.

title: Decide fate of Math Preview compatibility settings and commands
description:
## Summary
The orphaned Math Preview panel files have been removed, but compatibility no-op commands and configuration keys remain.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Review whether the disabled Math Preview commands in src/core/commands.ts should stay for compatibility or be removed
- Review whether latex-workshop.mathpreviewpanel.* settings in package.json should stay for compatibility or be deprecated/removed
- Update docs/changelog accordingly if compatibility surface changes

## Notes
This follow-up is intentionally separate from safe dead-file cleanup because removing settings or commands may break existing user configuration or automation.

title: Investigate macOS 26 Electron SIGABRT in test:ci
description: After stripping inherited VS Code/Electron environment from test/runTest.ts, the original Cannot find module '/Users/akira/LaTeX-Secure-Workspace/test/fixtures/unittest' failure is gone. The remaining local failure on macOS 26.3.1 is an Electron SIGABRT during NSApplication/RegisterApplication startup when @vscode/test-electron launches the VS Code 1.96.0 test app. Crash reports are written to ~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/Electron-*.ips. Investigate whether this is specific to integrated terminals, direct Electron launching on newer macOS releases, or a @vscode/test-electron compatibility issue, and restore a locally runnable test:ci path.
close_reason: Confirmed the reported macOS SIGABRT only occurred when the VS Code GUI test app was launched inside the sandbox. Outside the sandbox, the runner starts correctly; remaining test failures are tracked separately.

title: Investigate isolated self-host viewer SyncTeX test flake
description: The re-enabled forward SyncTeX integration suite is back in testground, but running only LATEXWORKSHOP_SUITE=05_viewer_synctex still intermittently aborts the VS Code self-host harness on macOS. Follow up on the harness instability separately from the runtime fix.

title: Smoke-test direct-mounted PDF custom editor in VS Code
close_reason: Direct-mounted PDF custom editor wiring has been restored and verified with npm run compile plus npm run test:ci. Follow-up viewer-specific integration coverage is tracked in LaTeX-Secure-Workspace-sky because test/suites/05_viewer.test.ts remains skipped.

title: Stabilize baseline lint and VS Code integration tests on master
Investigate remaining dependency vulnerabilities
Refine marketplace metadata and audit build-time scripts
description: Tune package.json/README for marketplace positioning around extension capabilities rather than project history, and audit build/package scripts for unexpected or unsafe behavior during build time. Validate compile/tests after changes.

title: Manually verify minimal PDF tab viewer UX before merge
Fix open code scanning alerts in completion and unified bundle
description: Resolve the remaining open GitHub code scanning alerts still present on the current branch: tighten the completion regex in src/completion/latex.ts to avoid ReDoS and patch the bundled resources/unified.js escaping sites flagged by CodeQL. Re-run compile/tests afterward and push the fixes.

title: Update security contact to security mailbox
description: Update SECURITY.md to use security@toppymicros.com as the coordinated disclosure contact and publish the documentation change.

title: Unskip PDF viewer integration coverage
description: The direct-mounted PDF custom editor path now builds and test:ci passes, but test/suites/05_viewer.test.ts is still skipped. Re-enable or replace the skipped viewer suite with a stable smoke test for the VS Code tab PDF viewer.

title: Fix missing unified runtime deps in published VSIX
description: Published releases v11.1.1 and current Marketplace install are missing @unified-latex runtime dependencies required by resources/unified.js. The extension fails activation with Cannot find module '@unified-latex/unified-latex-util-parse', which removes commands like latex-workshop.build and prevents the PDF viewer from loading. Restore the missing production dependencies in package.json/package-lock, verify packaged VSIX contents, and ship a fixed release.

title: Integrate vendored vscode-pdfviewer-secure runtime
description:
## Summary
Replace the current internal PDF tab viewer runtime with a maintainable vendor snapshot of thinksyncs/vscode-pdfviewer-secure while keeping LaTeX-Secure-Workspace specific commands and settings.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Vendor the secure PDF viewer runtime with provenance documentation
- Keep extension-specific glue separate from vendored upstream files
- Preserve tab-based PDF viewing through the existing latex-workshop.view flow
- Run compile and tests successfully after integration

## Notes
Favor a sync-friendly layout so future viewer updates can be applied with minimal local patching.

title: Track upstream @vscode/vsce yauzl advisory
Remove unreachable secure-build legacy files
description: Delete source files that are unreachable from the secure-build runtime graph and remove the unit test that only exercised the dead external-build helper.
close_reason: Removed the unreachable external-build helper, unused quick-pick helper, and the unit test that only exercised the dead path.

title: Stabilize multiroot build suite cleanup for custom recipe outputs
Clean repository base folder and refresh manual
close_reason: Removed unused extras, organized docs/manual, and cleaned local root artifacts.

title: Fix workflow guardrails and TeX Live lock logic
description: Repair the lock workflow API path, stop the security guardrail from self-matching pull_request_target, and make the TeX Live workflow naming/cache strategy match the actual installation source.
close_reason: Fixed the lock workflow API path, stopped the pull_request_target guardrail from self-matching, and aligned TeX Live workflow naming/cache docs with the current rolling repository.

title: Restore reverse SyncTeX for minimal PDF tab viewer
description: Forward SyncTeX is now wired for the minimal internal PDF tab viewer, but reverse SyncTeX (PDF -> TeX) is still not restored. Add a secure postMessage path from the webview back into the extension and re-enable click-based SyncTeX navigation without reviving the legacy websocket viewer.
